label: Global GDP Forecast 2026
contestNote: Reference scenario 3.1%; adverse scenario (war continuation) 2.5%; severe scenario 2.0%. Headline inflation 4.4% globally. Mid-East & Central Asia region cut 2pp to 1.9%.
deltaNote: From Jan 2026 baseline 3.3%; adverse/severe scenarios -0.8pp to -1.3pp from baseline

label: US Unemployment Rate
deltaNote: vs 2023 trough (3.4%); Feb revised down 130K to net -92K — first monthly payroll loss since 2020

label: US Core PCE Inflation
deltaNote: Jan 2026 BEA data; 3-month annualized rate 3.66% — re-acceleration above Fed 2% target on tariff pass-through

label: US 10Y Treasury Yield
source: Federal Reserve H.15 / FRED DGS10, Apr 10 2026
deltaNote: Apr 10 settle at 4.31% — down from 4.46% Apr 4 peak but elevated; core CPI miss (2.6% vs 2.7% forecast) provided mild bond relief; S&P +3% for week; inflation risk premium persists ahead of Apr 14 WEO and Apr 29–30 FOMC

label: US Consumer Confidence (CB Index)
deltaNote: Apr 2026 beat expectations (~89) — highest reading of 2026 — but Expectations Index 72.2 is still below 80 recession-warning threshold for the 15th consecutive month. Michigan Final Apr confirmed 47.6 all-time record low.

label: Michigan Consumer Sentiment (Apr Final)
deltaNote: ALL-TIME RECORD LOW CONFIRMED — Final April 2026 reading confirmed near 47.6 preliminary; below 1980 energy crisis nadir of 51.7. Year-ahead inflation expectations 4.8% (from 3.8% — largest one-month jump in survey history). Long-run 5Y expectations also elevated. FOMC April 28-29 meets with sentiment at all-time lows and Brent crude at $107/barrel.

label: S&P 500 / Market Risk Gauge
delta: Down from ATH ~7,165 (Apr 24 close) as Powell's final FOMC meeting + Warsh 13-11 committee vote + Brent $114 oil spike weigh on equities
deltaNote: S&P 500 fell 0.46% on Apr 28 (to ~7,138) on an OpenAI-revenue-miss-driven chip selloff; extended to ~7,128 on Apr 29 as mega-cap hyperscalers fell 1-2% ahead of after-close earnings (MSFT, META, GOOGL, AMZN — combined ~$11T market cap). FOMC holds at 3.50-3.75% — Powell's final meeting as Chair. Warsh advanced 13-11 party-line Senate Banking Committee vote (historic first), heads to full Senate floor vote. Brent crude at $114-116 (highest since June 2022). Q1 2026 GDP advance (BEA, 8:30 AM Apr 30; GDPNow 1.24%) + March PCE (also Apr 30) are next catalysts. Recession probabilities: Goldman Sachs 30%, JPMorgan 35%, Moody's Analytics 49%.

label: US Avg Effective Tariff Rate
source: Penn Wharton Budget Model, Apr 15 2026; CBP CAPE Portal, Apr 20 2026
contestNote: 8.9% overall under Section 122; China faces 31.6% effective rate (post-IEEPA Supreme Court ruling). CAPE portal launched Apr 20 for $166B in IEEPA refunds — system crashed under high volume on Day 1. Over 56,000 importers in queue across 53M eligible shipments. Section 122 rate faces ongoing Court of International Trade litigation; expiry in ~120 days restores to ~8.2%. Original IEEPA 104% China rate struck down Feb 20, 2026.

label: China GDP Q1 2026 (actual) / 2025 Full Year
contestNote: Q1 2026 beat (+5.0% vs 4.8% forecast) driven by Jan-Feb export front-loading. March exports fell sharply to +2.5% YoY (miss vs +8.6% forecast); factory-gate prices turned positive (+0.5%) for first time in 3+ years. Q2 headwinds mounting from Iran war demand destruction.
deltaNote: Q4 2025: 4.5%. Q1 2026: 5.0% — beat driven by front-loaded exports before Iran war demand shock took full effect. IMF maintains 2026 China forecast at ~4.5%.

label: Global Policy Uncertainty / Recession Risk
source: Baker-Bloom-Davis / FRED; Wall Street research Apr 15-17 2026
delta: Easing from Apr 10 peak on ceasefire/Hormuz reopening — but structural risks elevated
deltaNote: Goldman Sachs recession probability: 30%; JPMorgan: 35% (down sharply from 65% Apr 10 high); Moody's Analytics: ~42%; Bloomberg economists: 30%; NY Fed yield curve: 18.8%. Oil crash (Apr 17: -9.6%) improves inflation outlook for FOMC Apr 29-30. Core PCE 3.06%, Michigan 47.6 all-time low, and ECB expected to hike to 2.5% by year-end keep macro risks elevated.
